jokes.text: Love Contract commercial concept - formal sexual consent document
jokes.punchline: Treating sex like a business contract with clauses
jokes.explanation: Clever social commentary on consent culture and legal paranoia, executed as mock commercial

jokes.text: Just sign here and here... and initial there, for oral
jokes.punchline: Specifying oral sex in legal terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - the casual businesslike tone contrasts perfectly with intimate subject matter

jokes.text: And just initial there, if you decline anal
jokes.punchline: Escalating to more graphic sexual acts in legal jargon
jokes.explanation: Natural escalation but predictable - follows expected pattern

jokes.text: From the makers of The Love Contract comes: The Confidentiality Agreement
jokes.punchline: Now there's a sequel product for post-sex shame
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation that mirrors real infomercial structure while commenting on hookup culture

jokes.text: Or I'll sue your ass... Kobe!
jokes.punchline: Reference to Kobe Bryant's sexual assault case
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that lands the entire sketch's purpose - very current for 2003 audience

jokes.text: Available at all Wall-Barts
jokes.punchline: Wal-Mart mispronunciation suggests cheap, trashy product placement
jokes.explanation: Decent throwaway line that reinforces the sketch's low-class commercial parody

jokes.text: I seen, like... like a orange... his aura or whatever, I seen it, it was orange
jokes.setup: Charlie being starstruck by Rick James
jokes.punchline: Claiming to literally see Rick's spiritual aura
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Charlie's earnest street mysticism meets celebrity worship

jokes.text: I'm one of the baddest motherfuckers of all time. I'm one of the best singers and one of the best-lookin' motherfuckers you've ever seen
jokes.punchline: Comically over-the-top self-aggrandizement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - captures Rick's legendary ego and cocaine-fueled bravado

jokes.text: Hold my drink, bitch
jokes.setup: Rick about to do something outrageous
jokes.punchline: Casually ordering someone around before face-licking
jokes.explanation: Iconic line - perfectly economical and character-specific, became widely quoted

jokes.text: I'm Rick James, bitch
jokes.punchline: Iconic tagline that becomes the sketch's calling card
jokes.explanation: Cultural phenomenon - perfect economy, character integration, became one of TV's most quoted lines

jokes.text: Habitually... he's a habitual line-stepper
jokes.punchline: Formal phrase for describing Rick's outrageous behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant coinage - Charlie's street-smart psychology meets formal language, became widely quoted

jokes.text: Charlie Murphy! [SLAP] That was, cold-blooded...
jokes.setup: Rick calling Charlie's name at Studio 54
jokes.punchline: Unexpected slap followed by Rick's casual assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff - the unexpected violence followed by Rick's casual 'cold-blooded' assessment

jokes.text: Unity... [ring imprint description]
jokes.punchline: The irony of a peace message leaving a violent mark
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic visual - peace symbol becoming instrument of violence, perfectly crafted contradiction

jokes.text: This is a celebration, bitch
jokes.punchline: Calling violence a 'celebration'
jokes.explanation: Character-perfect justification - Rick's cocaine logic turning assault into party

jokes.text: Now, Darkness, the tables are turned
jokes.punchline: Formal dramatic language for a hotel room fight
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Rick's dramatic flair and the 'Darkness' nickname introduction

jokes.text: Bitch, come over here and have sex with Charlie Murphy
jokes.setup: Rick offering a truce after violence
jokes.punchline: Offering sex as conflict resolution
jokes.explanation: Classic Rick absurdity - treating women as party favors and sex as peacemaking

jokes.text: Yeah, Charlie whipped my ass in his dreams
jokes.punchline: Claiming Charlie only wins in fantasies
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Rick's delusional confidence even when clearly beaten

jokes.text: Cocaine is a hell of a drug
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about cocaine's effects
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase - perfect deadpan delivery, became cultural shorthand for drug-induced behavior

jokes.text: Nice place, nigga
jokes.setup: Rick arriving at Eddie's house uninvited
jokes.punchline: Casual compliment before trashing the place
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the couch destruction - Rick's fake politeness before mayhem

jokes.text: Because Eddie could buy another one. Fuck your couch, nigga. Buy another one, you rich motherfucker
jokes.punchline: Justifying vandalism with wealth logic
jokes.explanation: Another iconic line - perfectly captures Rick's cocaine logic and entitlement, widely quoted

jokes.text: Darknesses, Darknesses... 'cause of my complexion he used to call me 'Darkness'
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining Rick's nickname for him
jokes.punchline: Rick's casual racism disguised as friendly nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - shows Rick's casual boundary-crossing and racial dynamics

jokes.text: See, this is long before Wesley Snipes. Back then, we was the blackest niggas on the planet according to Rick James
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference to establish their blackness level
jokes.explanation: Solid cultural reference joke - using Wesley Snipes as darkness benchmark

jokes.text: See, I never just did things just to do them... Yeah, I remember grinding my feet on Eddie's couch [rewind and repeat]
jokes.setup: Rick claiming he doesn't do random destructive things
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by his admission of exactly that
jokes.explanation: Brilliant editing comedy - the rewind emphasizes Rick's complete lack of self-awareness, perfect ironic structure

jokes.text: you black, midnight evil motherfucker! Black magic, Darkness! You're all Darkness!
jokes.punchline: Elaborate racial insults while being pummeled
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Rick's insults become increasingly elaborate and mystical under duress

jokes.text: Fuck yo couch, nigga. [while being carried out with limp legs]
jokes.setup: Rick being thrown out after beating
jokes.punchline: Still defiant despite obvious defeat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Rick's unbreakable defiance even in total defeat

jokes.text: They shoulda never gave you niggas money! You don't know how to appreciate shit!
jokes.setup: Rick being the one causing property damage
jokes.punchline: Blaming the victims for not appreciating his destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant victim-blaming - Rick's complete inversion of responsibility, became widely quoted

jokes.text: yo, we just gave him some help. Bust his fuckin' ass and shit
jokes.punchline: Charlie's literal interpretation of 'help'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'help' - Charlie's street logic vs. Eddie's compassion

jokes.text: wow, that's O.J. Simpson! He has a big fuckin' head, man
jokes.punchline: Reducing O.J. to his head size
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Charlie's unfiltered observations about celebrities

jokes.text: Come on, baby, show me your titties I'm Rick James, do something!
jokes.setup: Rick commanding attention at the club
jokes.punchline: Using his celebrity to demand nudity
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent sleaziness - Rick's abuse of fame for sexual purposes

jokes.text: I wish I had more hands. So I could give those titties four thumbs down
jokes.punchline: Wanting more hands to give worse review
jokes.explanation: Creative insult structure - subverts expectation of sexual compliment into elaborate criticism

jokes.text: What did the five fingers say to the face? [pause] Slap!
jokes.punchline: The answer is violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure - became widely quoted, excellent economy and timing

jokes.text: First of all, you don't slap a man
jokes.punchline: Treating slapping as a gender/class violation
jokes.explanation: Good character wisdom - Charlie's street code about masculine fighting rules

jokes.text: That was weeks ago, motherfucker I was partying!
jokes.setup: Rick trying to justify the earlier slap
jokes.punchline: Treating assault as acceptable party behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Rick logic - using party atmosphere to justify violence, classic cocaine reasoning

jokes.text: [Second] Cocaine's a hell of a drug
jokes.punchline: Repeated catchphrase explaining everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - the catchphrase gains power through repetition and context

jokes.text: Cocaine's a hell of a drug.
jokes.setup: Rick realizing he forgot hitting Charlie
jokes.punchline: Second use of his signature excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reinforces the catchphrase and shows Rick's go-to explanation for everything. The repetition strengthens the impact.

jokes.text: Show Charlie Murphy your titties. I'm Rick James. The milk's gone bad!
jokes.punchline: Random milk observation during sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Classic Rick randomness - the non-sequitur milk comment during sexual demands

jokes.text: Can you imagine two grown men doing this?
jokes.setup: Charlie reflecting on the absurd situation
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on adult celebrity behavior
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Charlie's self-aware commentary on the ridiculousness

jokes.text: Charlie used my name to pick up girls
jokes.punchline: Charlie needed Rick's celebrity to get women
jokes.explanation: Decent ending - reinforces the power dynamic and Rick's celebrity privilege
